name: FastAPI
what_it_does: High-performance Python async web framework for building APIs with automatic OpenAPI/Swagger documentation generation using type hints and Pydantic validation.
use_cases: Building REST API backends that AI agents expose to orchestrators or other agents
Creating agent tool servers with auto-generated OpenAPI specs for dynamic tool discovery
Serving ML model inference endpoints with async request handling and type-validated inputs
Rapid prototyping of agent microservices with built-in validation and interactive documentation
Building structured agent-to-agent communication APIs with Pydantic-enforced contracts
not_for: Server-side rendered web applications with templates — use Django or Flask with Jinja2
Non-Python teams building APIs — use Express (Node.js), Gin (Go), or Spring Boot (Java)
Consuming external APIs — FastAPI is for building APIs, not calling them
best_when: You need a Python API backend with automatic OpenAPI spec generation, async performance, and Pydantic-validated request/response models that agents can discover and call.
avoid_when: You need a full-stack framework with built-in ORM, admin UI, and auth out of the box — Django covers those needs more completely.

auth.notes: Library — no auth required to install or use. FastAPI provides optional auth utilities (OAuth2PasswordBearer, HTTPBearer, APIKeyHeader) that developers opt into; the framework enforces nothing by default.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free and open source — MIT licensed, no usage limits
pricing.notes: FastAPI is MIT-licensed open source software. No vendor lock-in. Tiangolo (creator) offers consulting, but the framework itself is entirely free.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Returns structured 422 Unprocessable Entity with Pydantic error arrays (loc, msg, type) that agents can parse to auto-correct malformed requests. Best-in-class validation error format for agent consumption.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No built-in idempotency key support — developers must implement at the endpoint level. The framework is agnostic to idempotency concerns.
known_agent_gotchas: FastAPI builds APIs for agents to call — it is not itself an API that agents call; distinguish clearly when reading docs
Automatic OpenAPI spec at /openapi.json enables agents to discover and introspect FastAPI-built endpoints dynamically
Pydantic v2 (FastAPI 0.100+) has breaking syntax changes vs v1 — verify which version examples target before using
Background tasks (BackgroundTasks) complete after the HTTP response is sent — agents cannot assume async work is done when response arrives
CORS is not enabled by default — agents calling from browser contexts or cross-origin will fail silently without CORSMiddleware

security_score_components.security_notes: FastAPI provides OAuth2/API key utilities but enforces nothing — all security is opt-in by the developer. TLS handled by ASGI server (uvicorn) and reverse proxy, not the framework. Pydantic validation prevents injection via typed fields. fastapi-users adds production-ready auth patterns.
